content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

February 16, 1977

13-77-02-040

Janet Sandlin
Office of the Secretary of State
111 Capitol Mall, Room 208
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Janet:

This letter is to confirm the advice which the Commission staff provided to you concerning termination of "committees" as that term is used in Government Code Section 82013.
Initially, we observe that committees established pursuant to subsections (b) and (c) of Section 82013 generally terminate at the end of each year.

Committees established pursuant to Section 82013(a), on the other hand, do not automatically terminate at the end of each year. Presently, no formal procedure exists by which such a committee may terminate. However, the Commission staff has advised filing officers of an acceptable informal procedure consisting of two steps. First, the committee must eliminate all debts or obligations and dispose of all funds. Second, the committee treasurer should send to the Secretary of State and each officer who receives a copy of the committee's final campaign statement a letter which states that the committee has eliminated all debts or obligations, disposed of all funds and ceased to receive contributions or make expenditures and does not anticipate doing so in the future. The letter also should advise the recipients that future campaign statements will not be filed. If all of these steps are not completed, the committee will remain a committee even though, by virtue of inactivity, it may not possess any present reporting obligations.

At the Commission's March meeting, the staff will present a regulation establishing a formal procedure for terminating

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

Janet Sandlin
Page Two
February 16, 1977


Section 82013 committees. I will ask Jeanne Pritchard of our
staff to forward a copy of our proposed regulation to you. If
you have any comments or suggestions, you may contact me or
Natalie West.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim
Ted Prim
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

TP:jo

State of California

Memorandum

To : Gina Siciliano-Kutchins
     Fair Political Practices Commission

From : Secretary of State

Subject: TERMINATION OF COMMITTEES DURING A CAMPAIGN

Date: February 16, 1977

Please confirm our telephone conversation of last week in which
you conveyed the FPPC's staff opinion that committees would be
allowed to terminate during a campaign if they so indicated on
their disclosure statement or in a letter.

Our office is now accepting letters stating that there has been
no activity since the last filing to satisfy reporting requirements.
We strongly advise the filer to sign the letter under penalty of
perjury.

I look forward to receiving your letter confirming your advice by
early next week.

JANET E. SANDLIN, Auditor
Political Reform Division

JES/cs
